modmenu.descriptionTranslation.pishock-z: Connect PiShock devices to Minecraft! Like hardcore mode, but with real consequences. Zap yourself when you take damage or die!
modmenu.summaryTranslation.pishock-zap: Zap yourself with PiShock when you take damage.
description.pishock-zap.config.api.web_v: You can find your username and API key on the %1$s. Note that regenerating your API key will disconnect all other PiShock integrations which use it, so if you have one written down then use that. Share codes are available from the %2$s (click the "share" button to generate a share code).

This mod sends data to the PiShock servers. This data includes your username, API key, share codes, the log identifier, and the duration and intensity of all shock and vibrate operations. This data is sent as required when a shock or vibrate operation is requested. This data is only sent to the PiShock API and is not shared with any other service.
description.pishock-zap.config.api.local: Local Serial API is intended for advanced users only. It requires the PiShock device to be directly connected via USB to the computer running Minecraft. The advantage is that it provides near-instant feedback unlike the Web API which is constrained by network latency.

Shocker IDs are short numeric identifiers available from the %1$s. You can find them by clicking on the settings icon in the bottom-center of each individual controller. This is not the same as your PiShock device ID.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.general.vibra:
Only vibrate the device, don't shock, even on death.
This can be used to test the mod or simply
provide a less intense experience.
For example, if you want to check your share codes work.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.general.shock:
Shock the user on death with a
configurable intensity and duration.
If disabled, normal damage shocks will still occur.

Shock the user with an intensity
based on remaining health instead of damage.
This may encourage the user to stay healed up.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.durati: The duration of the shock/vibration by default
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.max_du:
The maximum duration of the shock/vibration
if the duration is accumulated
(See backoff settings)
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.vibrat:
How hard you have to get damaged to get shocked.
Anything below this will give a vibration.
Set to 0% to disable vibration, or
to 100% to save shocks for death only.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.min_da:
The amount of damage which will result in
the minimum intensity of shocks/vibrations.
Anything below this will be the minimum intensity.
This is 5% (usually half a heart) by default,
but can be changed if you have non-standard
max hp, or if you want to experience
lower intensities more often.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.max_da:
The amount of damage which will result in
the maximum intensity of shocks/vibrations.
Anything above this will be the maximum intensity.
This is 100% by default, but can be decreased
if you want to experience higher intensities
more often.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.vibrat:
The minimum intensity of the vibration
on damage

The maximum intensity of the vibration
on damage (when just at threshold)
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.limits.shock_:
The minimum intensity of shocks on damage
(when just above threshold)

The maximum intensity of shocks on damage
i.e. when the player takes the enough damage
to reach the "max shock intensity" threshold,
or the highest it can accumulate to via backoff

The intensity of shocks on death.
This can be above your usual maximum.

The duration of shocks on death
This can be above your usual maximum.
Which devices to activate on damage
Which devices to activate on death
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.debounce.accu:
Accumulate the duration of shocks/vibrations
If this is enabled, queued shocks/vibrations will be
combined and their durations added together.
Limits are of course still respected.
If weaker shocks are queued, they will
add less to the duration.

Accumulate the intensity of shocks/vibrations
If this is enabled, queued shocks/vibrations will be
combined and their intensities added together.
Limits are of course still respected.
This is similar to the effect of taking the damage all at once.
If this is disabled, the intensity of the resulting shock/vibration
will be the greatest of the queued shocks/vibrations.
Disabling it is easier on the user :)
If shock-on-health is enabled, this will be ignored.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.debounce.queu:
Queue differently-sized shocks/vibrations separately
If this is enabled, during backoff, differently-sized
shocks/vibrations will not accumulate duration with
each other, and will instead be sent separately.
Disables intensity accumulation.
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api_type: The type of API to use for PiShock communication
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.log_ident: The identifier to display in the PiShock logs
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.share_cod:
The share codes of the devices you want
to control with this mod
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.serial_po:
The path or name of the serial port to use
for local serial API communication
For example, COM3 or /dev/ttyUSB0
tooltip.pishock-zap.config.api.device_id: The IDs of the shockers to drive via local serial API
